title: Degree Adverbs
description: Master khá, tương đối, hơi, đến nỗi — expressing degrees from 'quite' to 'so much that'
intro: Beyond rất/lắm/quá, Vietnamese has finer degree words. From highest to lowest: rất > khá > tương đối > không...lắm > hơi. Plus 'đến nỗi/đến mức' for extreme consequences.

questions.prompt: Put these in order from STRONGEST to WEAKEST: rất, khá, tương đối, hơi
questions.explanation: rất (very) > khá (quite) > tương đối (relatively) > không...lắm (not very) > hơi (a little/rather).

questions.prompt: What does 'khá' mean as a degree word?
questions.explanation: 'Tiếng Anh của em ấy khá tốt' = Her English is quite good. Less than 'rất' but still positive.

questions.prompt: Vào buổi tối, con đường này ___ vắng vẻ. (This street is RELATIVELY deserted at night.)
questions.explanation: 'tương đối' = relatively. A moderate, measured assessment.

questions.prompt: Giá cả ở đây ___ cao. (The prices here are RATHER high.)
questions.explanation: 'hơi' = a little/rather. Often pairs with negative-connotation adjectives: hơi cao (rather high), hơi khó (rather difficult).

questions.prompt: What kind of adjectives does 'hơi' usually combine with?
questions.explanation: 'hơi' often pairs with negative-feeling words: hơi đắt (rather expensive), hơi khó chịu (a little uncomfortable).

questions.prompt: Bọn trẻ xem phim say sưa ___ quên ăn. (The kids watched so intently THAT they forgot to eat.)
questions.explanation: 'đến nỗi' = to such an extent that. Action was so intense it caused a consequence.

questions.prompt: What do 'đến nỗi' and 'đến mức' express?
questions.correct: Something so extreme it causes a consequence
options: Something so extreme it causes a consequence
questions.explanation: 'Trời nóng đến mức tôi không ngủ được' = It was so hot (to the point that) I couldn't sleep.

questions.prompt: Cô ta ___ xấu. (She is NOT TOO bad-looking.)
questions.explanation: 'không đến nỗi' + adj = not too bad. Has a positive undertone — better than expected.

questions.prompt: 'Không đến nỗi' usually conveys what feeling?
questions.correct: Positive — not as bad as expected
options: Positive — not as bad as expected
questions.explanation: 'không đến nỗi xấu' = not too bad-looking (better than you'd think). Softens a negative.
You cannot combine 'rất' with 'không đến nỗi'. 'rất không đến nỗi' is ungrammatical.

questions.prompt: Anh ấy ___ kiêu ngạo như mọi người nghĩ. (He is NOT AS arrogant as people thought.)
questions.explanation: 'không đến nỗi' = not as (bad as thought). Corrects a misconception.

questions.prompt: When the exact negative quality is NOT stated, what phrase replaces 'không đến nỗi + adj'?
questions.explanation: 'Anh ấy không đến nỗi nào' = He's not that bad (general, without specifying what). '-nào' replaces the adjective.

questions.prompt: Tiếng Anh của em ấy ___ tốt. (Her English is QUITE good.)
questions.explanation: 'khá tốt' = quite good. Moderate positive assessment, below 'rất' but above 'tương đối'.

questions.prompt: Tối qua, trời nóng ___ tôi không ngủ được. (Last night was so hot THAT I couldn't sleep.)
questions.explanation: Both 'đến nỗi' and 'đến mức' work here. They're interchangeable: 'to such a degree that'.

questions.prompt: To express a negative assessment (bad connotation), which is preferred over 'không đến nỗi'?
questions.explanation: 'không...lắm' for bad connotation: 'không khiêm tốn lắm' (not very humble). 'không đến nỗi' implies 'not as bad as thought' (positive).

questions.prompt: Tôi thấy ___ khó chịu. (I feel A LITTLE uncomfortable.)
questions.explanation: 'hơi' = a little. Softens the negative feeling. 'hơi khó chịu' = slightly uncomfortable.
